properties.Dedication_Signed: Governor Ritchie Highway
properties.Info: Named in honor of Albert C. Ritchie, 49th Governor of Maryland for a record four terms.

properties.Dedication_Signed: Aris T. Allen Boulevard
properties.Info: Named in honor of Dr. Aris T. Allen, who was the first African-American chair of the Maryland Republican party and the first African-American to run for a statewide office in Maryland.

properties.Dedication_Signed: Paul T. Pitcher Memorial Highway
properties.Info: Named in honor of Paul T. Pitcher, an Anne Arundel County Executive who originally conceived MD Route 100.

properties.Dedication_Signed: This Highway Dedicated to Senator John A. Cade
properties.Info: Dedicated to John A. Cade, former Maryland Senator who was instrumental in securing funding for I-97.

properties.Dedication_Signed: Generals Highway
properties.Info: Named in honor of General George Washington who traveled this route in December 1783 as part of his journey from New York to Annapolis to resign as Commander-in-Chief.

properties.Dedication_Signed: William Preston Lane Jr. Memorial Bridge
properties.Info: Named in honor of William Preston Lane, Jr., 52nd Governor of Maryland who in 1947 initiated the legislation to begin construction of the first span of the "Bay Bridge".

properties.Dedication_Signed: This Highway Dedicated to Governor Theodore R. McKeldin
properties.Info: Dedicated to Theodore Roosevelt McKeldin, 53rd Governor of Maryland. Construction of the "Baltimore Beltway" began under his watch in 1954.

properties.Dedication_Signed: John F. Kennedy Memorial Highway
properties.Info: Named in honor of President John F. Kennedy, who officiated the ceremony dedicating I-95 and was assassinated 8 days later.

properties.Dedication_Signed: Dedicated to Veterans of Foreign Wars
properties.Info: Dedicated to Veterans of Foreign Wars, a nonprofit veterans service organization devoted to veterans  service, legislative advocacy, and military and community service programs.

properties.Dedication_Signed: Francis Scott Key Bridge
properties.Info: Named in honor of Francis Scott Key, Maryland native and author of our national anthem, "The Star-Spangled Banner."

properties.Dedication_Signed: Maryland Purple Heart Trail I-95
properties.Info: Let it be known that those who wear the Purple Heart have given their blood in the defense of their homeland and shall be forever revered by their countrymen.

properties.Dedication_Signed: This Bridge Dedicated to Harry Dorsey Gough Founder of Perry Hall
properties.Info: Dedicated to Harry Dorsey Gough, influential 18th century businessman and original owner of the Perry Hall Mansion, the area’s namesake.

properties.Dedication_Signed: 175th Infantry Memorial Highway Dedicated to MD's "Dandy 5th"
properties.Info: Dedicated to the 175th Infantry Regiment, one of the oldest in the U.S. formed during the American Revolution and has a garrison in Dundalk. The “Dandy 5th” has won 24 battle honors spanning every major American war.

properties.Dedication_Signed: Vietnam Veterans Highway
properties.Info: Dedicated to Veterans of the Vietnam War (1959-1975). One of several dedications in Maryland honoring those who served.

properties.Info: Let it be known that those who wear the Purple Heart have given their blood in the defense of their homeland and shall be forever revered by their countrymen.

properties.Dedication_Signed: The Louis L. Goldstein Memorial Highway
properties.Info: Dedicated to Louis L. “Louie” Goldstein, Calvert County native and MD State Comptroller for a record 40 years, 1958-1998.

properties.Dedication_Signed: Dedicated to John V. Riffle
properties.Info: Dedicated to John V. Riffle, former Town of Greensboro Commissioner.

properties.Info: Dedicated to Frederick Douglass, Talbot County native, orator, writer and statesman who escaped slavery and became a national leader of the abolitionist movement.

properties.Info: Named in honor of Francis Scott Key, Maryland native and author of our national anthem, "The Star-Spangled Banner".

properties.Info: Dedicated to Walter Baker, prominent area political official who represented District 36 in the Maryland State Senate from 1979 to 2002.

properties.Info: Marked highways that pay tribute to those who have served in the U.S. Armed Forces. The blue star was used on service flags to denote a service member fighting in the war.

properties.Info: Named in honor of Joseph B. Biggs, affectionately known as “Joeby," a prominent Cecil County Commissioner in the 1970's.

properties.Dedication_Signed: Thomas J. Hatem Memorial Bridge
properties.Info: Named in honor of Thomas J. Hatem, longtime Harford County Democratic political figure who served a term in the MD House of Delegates and held other various public servant roles.

properties.Info: Dedicated to United States Marine Corps Staff Sergeant James M. Malachowski, Carroll County native, who was killed in action in the Helmand province of Afghanistan on March 20, 2011 while supporting Operation Enduring Freedom.

properties.Dedication_Signed: I-81 Bridge Dedicated to US Navy Fireman Patrick Howard Roy US Navy Seaman Craig Brian Wibberley Killed Aboard USS Cole October 12, 2000
properties.Info: Dedicated to United States Navy Fireman Apprentice Patrick Howard Roy and United States Navy Seaman Apprentice Craig Brian Wibberley, Washington County natives who were among the 17 Sailors killed in action on October 12, 2000 during the terrorist attack on the U.S.S. Cole in Yemen.

properties.Dedication_Signed: The Governor Harry R. Hughes Bridge
properties.Info: Named in honor of Harry Roe Hughes, 57th Governor of Maryland for two terms, 1979-1987 who served as the state's first Secretary of Transportation from 1971-1979.

properties.Dedication_Signed: Dedicated to Deputy Lawrence H. McParlin Charles County Sheriff's Office
properties.Info: Dedicated to Charles County Sheriff's Office Deputy Lawrence H. McParlin who was killed in the line of duty serving a court summons on May 21, 1918; believed to be the first officer from the Charles County Sheriff's Office to perish in the line of duty.

properties.Dedication_Signed: Dedicated to Ofc. Luke T. Hoffman Montgomery County Police Dept
properties.Info: Dedicated to Montgomery County Police Officer Luke T. Hoffman who was killed in the line of duty in pursuit of a suspected drunk driver on April 25, 2007.

properties.Dedication_Signed: Dedicated to S01 (SEAL) Patrick D. Feeks United States Navy
properties.Info: Dedicated to Untied States Navy Special Warfare Operator First Class Seal Patrick D. Feeks, Edgewater native who was killed in the line of duty on August 16, 2012 northeast of Kandahar, Afghanistan while supporting Operation Enduring Freedom.

properties.Dedication_Signed: SGT Michael Heede Jr USMC Afghanistan Campaign Killed In Action 7-13-09
properties.Info: Dedicated to United States Marine Corps Sergeant Michael W. Heede, Jr who was killed in action in the Helmand province of Afghanistan on July 13, 2009 while supporting Operation Enduring Freedom.

properties.Dedication_Signed: Dedicated to Henry Highland Garnet
properties.Info: Dedicated to Henry Highland Garnet, Kent County native, who was an abolitionist, minister, educator and orator and escaped slavery as a child. His "Call to Rebellion" speech in 1843 encouraged slaves to free themselves by rising up against owners.

properties.Dedication_Signed: This Parkway Dedicated to J. Glenn Beall Jr. United States Senator
properties.Info: Dedicated to John Glenn Beall, Jr. Cumberland native and longtime Maryland politician, who was instrumental in securing funding for Interstate 68 and Canal Place tourism development.

properties.Dedication_Signed: Dedicated to USMC Master Sergeant William "Bull" Evans
properties.Info: Dedicated to United States Marine Corps Master Sergeant William "Bull" Evans, Cresaptown native and decorated World War II and Korean War veteran, whose heroic acts made him a legend among Marines.

properties.Dedication_Signed: Ali Ghan Road
properties.Info: Named in honor of the Ali Ghan Temple, a Shrine that opened in 1938, serving a collateral body of Freemasonry.

properties.Dedication_Signed: Thomas B. Finan Memorial Bridge
properties.Info: Named in honor of Thomas B. Finan, Cumberland native and Maryland Attorney General from 1961-1966.

properties.Dedication_Signed: Anne Arundel County Veterans Memorial Bridge
properties.Info: Dedicated as Anne Arundel County Veterans Memorial Bridge.

properties.Dedication_Signed: Maryland World War II Memorial
properties.Info: Memorial to the 6,454 Marylanders who died in World War II - their names are inscribed in granite slabs surrounded by columns in this amphitheater overlooking the Severn River. Stone panels and mosaic circles further depict Maryland's role in the War.

properties.Dedication_Signed: Dedicated to CPL Jamel Leon Clagett Charles County Sheriff's Office
properties.Info: Dedicated to Charles County Sheriff’s Office Corporal Jamel Leon Clagett who was killed while driving home after work in a single vehicle crash on December 21, 2014.

properties.Dedication_Signed: Bridge Dedicated to SGT Kendell K. Frederick United States Army

properties.Dedication_Signed: SPC Erik W Hayes US Army Iraq Campaign Killed In Action 11-29-04 | Scenic Monocacy River

properties.Dedication_Signed: CPL Dale A. Burger Jr. USMC Iraq Campaign Killed In Action 11-14-04
properties.Info: Dedicated to United States Marine Corps Corporal Dale Alan Burger, Jr., Maryland native, who was killed in action in Anbar Province, Iraq on November 14, 2004 while supporting Operation Iraqi Freedom.

properties.Dedication_Signed: Bridge Dedicated to SSG Christopher W Swanson United States Army
properties.Info: Dedicated to United States Army Staff Sergeant Christopher W. Swanson, Arundel native, who was killed in action on July 22, 2006 in Al Ramadi, Iraq while supporting Operation Iraqi Freedom.

properties.Dedication_Signed: US Naval Academy Bridge
properties.Info: Named in honor of the Naval Academy's 150th anniversary.

properties.Dedication_Signed: Bridge Dedicated to William J “Duck” Bradley Jr.
properties.Info: Dedicated to William J. “Duck” Bradley, Jr., Oxford native and lifelong resident who was the MDOT State Highway Administration Project Engineer for several local area bridges.

properties.Dedication_Signed: Brooks Robinson Drive
properties.Info: Named in honor of Brooks Calbert Robinson, Jr., National Baseball Hame of Fame inductee in 1983, who played his entire 23-year major league  career for the Baltimore Orioles.

properties.Dedication_Signed: SFC Lance Vogeler US Army (Ranger) Afghanistan Killed In Action 10-1-10
properties.Info: Dedicated to United States Army Sergeant First Class Lance Herman Vogeler, Frederick native, who was killed in action in Afghanistan on October 1, 2010 while supporting Operation Enduring Freedom.

properties.Dedication_Signed: SGT Alessandro Carbonaro USMC Iraq Campaign Killed In Action 5-10-06
properties.Info: Dedicated to United States Marine Corps Sergeant Alessandro Carbonaro, Bethesda native, who was killed in action in Anbar Province, Iraq on May 10, 2006 while supporting Operation Iraqi Freedom.

properties.Dedication_Signed: This Highway Dedicated to Gold Star Mothers
properties.Info: Dedicated to the ongoing efforts of the Gold Star Mothers, a national group founded in 1928 dedicated to providing support and resources to mothers who have lost children during military service.

properties.Dedication_Signed: Bridge Dedicated to CPL William Kyle Ferrell United States Marine Corps
properties.Info: Dedicated to United States Marine Corps Corporal William Kyle Ferrell who was struck and killed on US-15 while assisting a disabled motorist.

properties.Dedication_Signed: Dedicated as the Pearl Harbor Memorial Bridge
properties.Info: Dedicated on the 65th anniversary of the attack on Pearl Harbor, the Severn River Bridge was renamed in a ceremony on December 7, 2006.

properties.Dedication_Signed: American Legion Memorial Bridge
properties.Info: Dedicated as the American Legion Memorial Bridge on May 30, 1969; originally named the "Cabin John Bridge."

properties.Dedication_Signed: Battle of North Point War of 1812
properties.Info: The Battle of North Point, fought on September 12, 1814, was a pivotal moment in the War of 1812. One of several commemorations in the immediate area.

properties.Dedication_Signed: Blue Star Memorial Highway
properties.Info: Marked highways that pay tribute to those who have served in the U.S. Armed Forces. The blue star was used on service flags to denote a service member fighting in the war.

properties.Info: Dedicated to United States Marine Corps Captain Jesse Melton III who was killed in action in the Parwan province of Afghanistan on September 9, 2008 while supporting Operation Enduring Freedom.

properties.Dedication_Signed: Dedicated to LCPL Norman "Wally" Anderson III CPL Joshua David Snyder United States Marine Corps
properties.Info: Dedicated to United States Marine Corps Lance Corporal Norman "Wally" Anderson III and Corporal Joshua David Snyder, Hereford High alumni and boot camp roommates, were killed in action on October 19, 2005 and November 30, 2005 respectively while supporting Operation Iraqi Freedom.

properties.Dedication_Signed: In memory of Cpl. Courtney Brooks Maryland Transportation Authority Police
properties.Info: Dedicated to Maryland Transportation Authority Police Corporal Courtney G. Brooks who was killed in the line of duty on January 1, 2008.

properties.Dedication_Signed: Dedicated to Cpl Glenn Hilliard Wicomico Co Sheriffs Office
properties.Info: Cpl. Glenn Hilliard, killed in the line of duty June 12, 2022, served the Wicomico Co Sheriff’s Office, the Berlin Police Dept, the Crisfield Police Dept & the Ocean City Police Dept during his career. He started in law enforcement as Student Security Personnel while enrolled at the University of Maryland Eastern Shore, a historically Black university in Princess Anne.

properties.Dedication_Signed: Harry W. Kelley Memorial Bridge
properties.Info: Named in honor of former Ocean City Mayor Harry W. Kelley who served for eight terms.

properties.Dedication_Signed: Governor Thomas Johnson Bridge
properties.Info: Named in honor of Thomas Johnson, 1st Governor of Maryland and Calvert County native.

properties.Dedication_Signed: Governor Thomas Johnson Bridge
properties.Info: Named in honor of Thomas Johnson, 1st Governor of Maryland and Calvert County native.

properties.Dedication_Signed: Governor Harry W. Nice Memorial Bridge
properties.Info: Named in honor of Harry Whinna Nice, 50th Governor of Maryland from 1935-1939.

properties.Dedication_Signed: Dedicated to Sheriff Deputy 1st Class Brian Keith Heller
properties.Info: Dedicated to Worcester County Sheriff's Office Deputy First Class Brian Keith Heller who was killed in the line of duty on June 14, 2000.

properties.Dedication_Signed: Dedicated to TFC Eric D. Monk TFC Edward A. Plank Jr.
properties.Info: Dedicated to Maryland State Police Trooper First Class Eric Dwight Monk and Trooper First Class Edward Plank both of whom were killed in the line of duty on April 9, 1988 and October 17, 1995, respectively.

properties.Dedication_Signed: Verrazano Bridge
properties.Info: Named in honor of Giovanni da Verrazano, Italian navigator, who in 1524 searched the Atlantic coastline of North America in vain for a route to Asia. Some historians believe he came ashore nearby.

properties.Dedication_Signed: Dedicated to Norman G. Myers, Sr.
properties.Info: Dedicated to Norman G. Myers, Sr., prominent business owner and longtime community leader and activist, often referred to as the "Mayor of Odenton.”

properties.Dedication_Signed: Hall Highway
properties.Info: Named in honor of Dr. Willard F. Hall of Edward W. McCready Hospital; the bridge crossing the Little Annemessex River was dedicated to him on December 17, 1922 and has been subsequently replaced in 1966.

properties.Dedication_Signed: The Nutwell Bridge | This Bridge is Dedicated In Honor of The Nutwell Family Whose Devotion to Civic Duty and Public Affairs Has Marked the History of Maryland For More Than 300 Years
properties.Info: Dedicated to the Nutwell family, who settled in the area in the 17th century.

properties.Dedication_Signed: The Chester River Bridge Dedicated to the Ex-Service Men of Kent County and Queen Anne's County
properties.Info: The Chester River Bridge Dedicated to the Ex-Service Men of Kent County and Queen Anne's County

properties.Dedication_Signed: The Chester River Bridge Dedicated to the Ex-Service Men of Kent County and Queen Anne's County
properties.Info: The Chester River Bridge Dedicated to the Ex-Service Men of Kent County and Queen Anne's County

properties.Dedication_Signed: Dedicated as Cort Memorial Bridge
properties.Info: Dedicated as Cort Memorial Bridge

properties.Dedication_Signed: Barbara Fritchie Bridge
properties.Info: Barbara Fritchie, a Unionist and central figure in Frederick’s history, was immortalized in the eponymous poem titled “Barbara Frietchie” by John Greenleaf Whittier (using the German spelling of her name.)

properties.Dedication_Signed: C. Philip Nichols Memorial Bridge
properties.Info: Dedicated to C. Philip Nichols, Sr. who served two terms in the MD House of Delegates from 1954-1962 and was instrumental in this bridge’s construction.

properties.Dedication_Signed: This Highway Dedicated to John S. Arnick
properties.Info: Dedicated to John S. Arnick, Baltimore native and US Marine Corps veteran, who served three separate spans in the MD House of Delegates ranging from 1969 through 2006.

properties.Dedication_Signed: Dedicated as Henrietta Lacks Way
properties.Info: Dedicated to Henrietta Lacks whose cervical cancer cells were the source of the HeLa cell line, the first immortalized cell line. The HeLa cell line continues to be a source of invaluable medical data to the present day.

properties.Dedication_Signed: In Memory of Nathan A. Blizzard
properties.Info: Dedicated to Nathan A. Blizzard who perished in an automobile accident at age 20 on his way to work on a dairy farm on December 5, 1997.

properties.Dedication_Signed: This Travel Information Center is named in honor of Governor Harry R. Hughes...
properties.Info: Named in honor of Harry Roe Hughes, 57th Governor of Maryland for two terms, 1979-1987 who served as the state's first Secretary of Transportation from 1971-1979.

properties.Dedication_Signed: Dedicated to Senator James Clark, Jr.
properties.Info: Dedicated to James Clark, Jr., Ellicott City native and former president of the MD State Senate from 1979-1983.

properties.Dedication_Signed: In Memory of Ofc. Duke G. Aaron III Maryland Transportation Authority Police
properties.Info: Dedicated to Maryland Transportation Authority Police Officer Duke G. Aaron III, who was killed in the line of duty when his patrol car was struck from behind on July 20, 2004.

properties.Dedication_Signed: Father John Stanislaus Cuddy Memorial Bridge
properties.Info: Named in honor of Father John Stanislaus Cuddy, a popular local priest at nearby St. Martins, who was killed in 1928 when a train struck his automobile. The incident spurred the bridge construction for safer crossing.

properties.Dedication_Signed: Clara Barton Parkway
properties.Info: Named in honor of Clara Barton, founder of the American Red Cross, who spent her final 15 years at her home in nearby Glen Echo which doubled as an early headquarters of the organization.

properties.Dedication_Signed: Dedicated to James G. Barrett
properties.Info: Dedicated to James G. Barrett, former Worcester County Commissioner, who advocated for the dualization of US-113.

properties.Dedication_Signed: This Parkway Dedicated In Memory of John P. Tawes
properties.Info: Dedicated to John P. Tawes, who advocated for the construction of a dual carriageway through the main section of Crisfield.

properties.Dedication_Signed: This Highway Dedicated to Senator Ida G. Ruben
properties.Info: Dedicated to Ida G. Ruben who represented District 20 in both the MD House of Delegates and MD Senate from 1975 to 2007 and advocated for the expansion of the FDA campus in White Oak.

properties.Dedication_Signed: This Bridge Dedicated to Senator Leo E. Green
properties.Info: Dedicated to Leo E. Green, who represented Prince George's County in the MD Senate from 1983 to 2007.

properties.Dedication_Signed: Dedicated to Police Officer III Noah A Leotta | Don't Drink And Drive
properties.Info: Dedicated to Montgomery County Police Officer Noah A. Leotta who was killed in the line of duty while pulling over a suspected drunk driver on December 3, 2015. As a result, Senate Bill 945 was passed, colloquially referred to as “Noah’s Law."

properties.Dedication_Signed: George Washington Highway
properties.Info: Named in honor of George Washington, the 1st President of the United States.

properties.Dedication_Signed: C. Philip Nichols Memorial Bridge
properties.Info: Dedicated to C. Philip Nichols, Sr. who served two terms in the MD House of Delegates from 1954-1962 and was instrumental in this bridge’s construction.

properties.Dedication_Signed: Dedicated to Cpl. Scott A. Wheeler Howard County Police Dept
properties.Info: Dedicated to Howard County Police Corporal Scott A. Wheeler who was killed in the line of duty while initiating a traffic stop on June 18, 2007.

properties.Dedication_Signed: Judge Lloyd L "Hot Dog" Simpkins Rest Area
properties.Info: Named in honor of Judge Lloyd L. “Hot Dog” Simpkins, lifelong Somerset County resident, who retired in 1990 at the age of 70 but continued to preside over cases on a part-time basis until 2010, making him the oldest sitting judge in Maryland history.

properties.Dedication_Signed: The U.S. Route 13 Travel Information Center is Dedicated to the Memory of Mark O. Pilchard
properties.Info: Dedicated to Mark O. Pilchard who served the citizens of the Lower Eastern Shore as a member of the MD House of Delegates from 1959-1966 and 1979-1989 and was instrumental in the creation of the rest area.

properties.Dedication_Signed: Dedicated to Senator Philip C. Jimeno
properties.Info: Dedicated to Philip C. Jimeno who represented District 31 for 28 years, serving terms in both the MD House of Delegates and Senate from 1979 to 2007.

properties.Dedication_Signed: Harriet Elizabeth Brown Memorial Parkway
properties.Info: Named in honor of Harriett Elizabeth Brown, Calvert County school teacher who fought for equal pay regardless of race and became the catalyst for the Maryland Teachers Pay Equalization Law, the first equalization law in Maryland.

properties.Dedication_Signed: Dedicated to Delegate Tyras Bunk Athey
properties.Info: Dedicated to Tyras Snowden “Bunk” Athey, Burtonsville native, former MD Secretary of the State who served in the MD House of Delegates from 1967 to 1993.

properties.Dedication_Signed: Dedicated to Dr Charles I Ecker
properties.Info: Dedicated to Charles Isaac Ecker, Maryland native, a career public school administrator and County Executive of Howard County from 1990 to 1998.

properties.Dedication_Signed: Dedicated to Paul Kerman
properties.Info: Dedicated to Paul Kerman who developed Drum Point Beach and served on the Maryland state judicial selection commission.

properties.Dedication_Signed: In Memory of a Fallen Hero Sgt. Wallace J. Mowbray Maryland State Trooper in the line of duty August 10, 1975
properties.Info: Dedicated to Maryland State Police Trooper Sergeant Wallace Johnson Mowbray who was killed in the line of duty during a traffic stop on August 10, 1975.

properties.Dedication_Signed: Bridge Dedicated to Capt. J.E. Daly Jr. | Cpl J.M. Frontczak
properties.Info: Dedicated to Montgomery County Police Captain James Edward Daly, Jr. and Corporal John Michael Frontczak who were killed in the line of duty attempting to arrest a robbery suspect on March 28, 1976.

properties.Dedication_Signed: This Highway Dedicated to Senator Charles H. Smelser
properties.Info: Dedicated to Charles H. “Bus” Smelser, Uniontown native who represented Carroll and Frederick counties in the MD House of Delegates from 1955 to 1963 and the MD Senate from 1967 to 1994.

properties.Dedication_Signed: This Highway Dedicated to Senator Charles H. Smelser
properties.Info: Dedicated to Charles H. “Bus” Smelser, Uniontown native who represented Carroll and Frederick counties in the MD House of Delegates from 1955 to 1963 and the MD Senate from 1967 to 1994.

properties.Dedication_Signed: Dedicated to Charles W Elgin Sr
properties.Info: Dedicated to Charles W. Elgin Sr., Poolesville native, who was a community leader and third generation Poolesville resident, served as the Town of Poolesville Commissioner from 1980 to 1994 and considered by many the “father” of Poolesville.

properties.Dedication_Signed: Dedicated to Peace Officer Christopher Nicholson
properties.Info: Dedicated to Smithsburg Police Officer Christopher Shane Nicholson who was killed in the line of duty on December 19, 2007.

properties.Dedication_Signed: Dedicated to OFC. William D. "Bill" Talbert  Montgomery County Police Department
properties.Info: Dedicated to Montgomery County Police Officer William D. “Bill” Talbert who was severely injured in the line of duty when struck by a drunk driver and ultimately perished from related complications several years later.

properties.Dedication_Signed: In Memory of Trooper John E. Sawa Trooper Larry E. Small Maryland State Police
properties.Info: Dedicated to Maryland State Police Trooper First Class John Edward Sawa and Trooper Larry Eugene Small who were killed in the duty when a tractor trailer driven by a drunk driver struck their parked patrol car on March 10, 1987.

properties.Dedication_Signed: Dedicated to Senator Leonard H. Teitelbaum Maryland State Senate
properties.Info: Dedicated to Leonard H. Teitelbaum who represented District 19 in both the MD House of Delegates and MD Senate from 1987 to 2007.

properties.Dedication_Signed: Dedicated to Governor Robert L. Ehrlich, Jr.
properties.Info: Dedicated to Robert L. Ehrlich, Jr., 60th Governor of Maryland, who prioritized construction of the Intercounty Connector (MD-200) during his administration. This all-electronic toll road opened in 2011 and planning efforts date back to the 1950’s.

properties.Dedication_Signed: Dedicated to Governor Robert L. Ehrlich, Jr.
properties.Info: Dedicated to Robert L. Ehrlich, Jr., 60th Governor of Maryland, who prioritized construction of the Intercounty Connector (MD-200) during his administration. This all-electronic toll road opened in 2011 and planning efforts date back to the 1950’s.

properties.Dedication_Signed: Dedicated to Capt. Joseph A. Mattingly Montgomery County Police Department
properties.Info: Dedicated to Montgomery County Police Captain Joseph Aloysius Mattingly who was killed in the line of duty when his patrol car struck a tree on September 13, 2003.

properties.Dedication_Signed: Dedicated to Firefighter Christopher A. Staley Cobb Island Volunteer Fire Dept
properties.Info: Dedicated to Cobb Island Volunteer Firefighter Christopher Allen Staley who died in the line of duty on August 6, 2011 attempting to save a distressed swimmer who also passed.

properties.Dedication_Signed: Dedicated to Capt. Joseph Kroboth, Jr. Halfway Volunteer Fire Co
properties.Info: Dedicated to Volunteer Fire Company of Halfway Captain Joseph Kroboth, Jr. who was killed in the line of duty on May 3, 1998 while directing traffic at the scene of a motor vehicle collision.

properties.Dedication_Signed: Dedicated to Delegate David G. Boschert
properties.Info: Dedicated to David G. Boschert, who served the citizens of Anne Arundel County both as a County Councilman and in the MD House of Delegates and advocated for the expansion of MD-32.

properties.Dedication_Signed: Dedicated to Senator Laurence Levitan Maryland State Senate
properties.Info: Dedicated to Laurence Levitan who represented District 15 in both the MD House of and MD Senate for over two decades. Senator Levitan was also the Chair of the Budget and Taxation Committee from 1979 to 1994.

properties.Dedication_Signed: Dedicated to Del. K. Bennett Bozman Maryland House of Delegates
properties.Info: Dedicated to K. Bennett Bozman who advocated for the dualization of US-113 and represented Wicomico and Worcester Counties in the MD House of Delegates from 1991 until his death in 2006.

properties.Dedication_Signed: Dedicated to TFC Anthony Jones Maryland State Police
properties.Info: Dedicated to Maryland State Police Trooper First Class Anthony Jones who was killed in the line of duty when he was struck by a vehicle operated by an intoxicated driver on May 9, 2004.

properties.Dedication_Signed: Dedicated to Sgt. Anthony M. Walker Prince George's County Police Dept
properties.Info: Dedicated to Prince George’s County Police Sergeant Anthony “Tony” Michael Walker, President of Prince George’s County Fraternal Order of Police, who succumbed to injuries sustained while on patrol in an automobile accident on November 15, 2003.

properties.Dedication_Signed: Dedicated to Corporal Thomas P. Jensen Prince George's County Police Dept.
properties.Info: Dedicated to Prince George’s County Police Corporal Thomas “Tommy” Paul Jensen, who succumbed to injuries sustained in an automobile accident while responding to a burglary in progress on March 9, 2010.

properties.Dedication_Signed: Dedicated to Sergeant Richard S. Findley Prince George's County Police Dept.
properties.Info: Dedicated to Prince George’s County Police Sergeant Richard Scott Findley, who was killed in the line of duty when he was intentionally struck by the driver of a stolen automobile on June 27, 2008.

properties.Dedication_Signed: Dedicated to Thomas M. Yeager
properties.Info: Dedicated to Thomas Merle Yeager, who was a member of the Howard County Council from 1975 to 1983 and represented newly formed District 13 in the MD Senate from 1983 to 1994.

properties.Dedication_Signed: Dedicated As Alfred B. Hilton Memorial Bridge
properties.Info: Dedicated to Alfred B. Hilton, Havre de Grace native, who was one of 16 African-American Union Army Soldiers who received the Medal of Honor for their service during the Civil War and the only service member from Harford County.

properties.Dedication_Signed: Dedicated to Delegate Norman H Conway
properties.Info: Dedicated to Norman H. Conway, Salisbury native and former President of Salisbury City Council (1982-1987), who represented District 38B in the MD House of Delegates from 1987 to 2015.

properties.Dedication_Signed: Dedicated to SSGT Charles I. Cartwright Untied States Marine Corps
properties.Info: Dedicated to United States Marine Corps Staff Sergeant Charles I. Cartwright who was killed in action in Farah province, Afghanistan on November 7, 2009 while supporting Operation Enduring Freedom.

properties.Dedication_Signed: Key Highway
properties.Info: Named in honor of Francis Scott Key, Maryland native and author of our national anthem, "The Star-Spangled Banner."

properties.Dedication_Signed: Broening Highway
properties.Info: Named in honor of William Frederick Broening, former Mayor of Baltimore for two terms, 1919-1923 and 1927-1931.

properties.Dedication_Signed: Dedicated to J. Frank Raley, Jr. Founder of Modern St. Mary's County
properties.Info: Dedicated to J. Frank Raley, Jr. who represented St. Mary’s County in the both the MD House of Delegates (1955-1959) and MD Senate (1963-1966) but never stopped advocating for the area. Among other things, Raley is credited with helping establish St. Mary’s College of Maryland as a four-year liberal arts college and was a driving force in the construction of the Thomas Johnson bridge.

properties.Dedication_Signed: Watermen's Memorial Bridge
properties.Info: Partial inscription from the neighboring Maryland Watermen’s Monument: Dedicated to all watermen in the state of Maryland for their tremendous contribution to our seafood industry, culture and heritage. We pay homage to their unique way of life and may their legacy forever be remembered.

properties.Dedication_Signed: Battle of North Point War of 1812
properties.Info: The Battle of North Point, fought on September 12, 1814, was a pivotal moment in the War of 1812. One of several commemorations in the immediate area.

properties.Dedication_Signed: Bridge Dedicated to Senator Gwendolyn Greene Britt
properties.Info: Dedicated to Gwendolyn Britt nee Greene, Washington D.C. native and civil rights activist who represented District 47 in the MD Senate from 2003 until her death in 2008 and fought for funding for the construction of CSX bridge over Annapolis Road.

properties.Dedication_Signed: In Memory Of Our Fallen Heroes TFC William P. Mills Jr. TFC Mark P. Groner Maryland State Troopers
properties.Info: Dedicated to Maryland State Police Trooper First Class William P. Mills, Jr. and Trooper First Class Mark P. Groner both of whom were killed in the line of duty on June 8, 1979 and October 1, 1992, respectively.

properties.Dedication_Signed: SPC Erik W Hayes US Army Iraq Campaign Killed In Action 11-29-04 | Scenic Monocacy River
properties.Info: Dedicated to United States Army Specialist Erik W. Hayes, Harney and Thurmont native, who was killed in action in Al Miqdadiyah, Iraq on November 29, 2004 while supporting Operation Iraqi Freedom.

properties.Dedication_Signed: Stephanie Roper Highway
properties.Info: Dedicated to Stephanie Roper, whose 1982 murder was the catalyst for the creation of a number of victim’s assistance services. It prompted her parents,  Roberta and Vincent Roper, to form the Maryland Crime Victims’ Resource Center, formerly known as the Stephanie Roper Committee and Foundation.

properties.Dedication_Signed: Stephanie Roper Highway
properties.Info: Dedicated to Stephanie Roper, whose 1982 murder was the catalyst for the creation of a number of victim’s assistance services. It prompted her parents,  Roberta and Vincent Roper, to form the Maryland Crime Victims’ Resource Center, formerly known as the Stephanie Roper Committee and Foundation.

properties.Dedication_Signed: This Interchange Dedicated to Bishop James R. Peebles Sr.
properties.Info: Dedicated to Bishop James R. Peebles, Sr., who formed nearby Jericho City of Praise with his wife in 1969.

properties.Dedication_Signed: This road dedicated in the memory of David A. Bramble
properties.Info: Dedicated to David A. Bramble, Fairlee native, who founded the construction company David A. Bramble, Inc., one of the largest employers in the upper Eastern Shore area.

properties.Dedication_Signed: Dedicated to DFC James Yvorra
properties.Info: Dedicated to Prince George's Fire Department Deputy Fire Chief James G. Yvorra who was killed in the line of duty when he was struck by an automobile while on the scene of an accident on January 28, 1988.

properties.Dedication_Signed: Bridge Dedicated to CPL Nicholas Lee Ziolkowski United States Marine Corps
properties.Info: Dedicated to United States Marine Corps Corporal Nicholas Lee Ziolkowski, Towson native, who was killed in action on November 14, 2004 by enemy fire in Fallujah, Iraq while supporting Operation Iraqi Freedom.

properties.Dedication_Signed: Dedicated to Maryland Natural Resources Police
properties.Info: Dedicated to the Maryland Natural Resources Police, Maryland’s oldest state police force and one of the nation’s oldest conservation law enforcement agencies, which celebrated 150 years of service in 2018.

properties.Dedication_Signed: Dedicated to Senator Norman Stone
properties.Info: Dedicated to Senator Norman R. Stone, Jr., Baltimore native, who represented District 6 in the in the Maryland State Senate for 12 terms from 1967 to 2015. Senator Stone is the longest serving senator in Maryland history.

properties.Dedication_Signed: SSGT William J Kundrat USMC (Raider) Killed 07-10-17
properties.Info: Dedicated to United States Marine Corps Staff Sergeant William Joseph Kundrat, Frederick native, who was killed in action during a military training accident in Mississippi on July 10, 2017.

properties.Dedication_Signed: Bridge Dedicated to Speaker of the House R Clayton Mitchell Jr
properties.Info: Dedicated to R. Clayton "Clay" Mitchell Jr., Chestertown native, who represented District 36 in the MD House of Delegates from 1971 to 1992 and was Speaker of the House from 1987 to 1993.

properties.Dedication_Signed: CPL Kirk J Bosselmann USMC Iraq Campaign Killed In Action 11-27-04
properties.Info: Dedicated to United States Marine Corps Corporal Kirk J. Bosselmann, Dickerson native, who was killed in action in Anbar Province, Iraq on November 27, 2004 while supporting Operation Iraqi Freedom.

properties.Dedication_Signed: Bridge Dedicated to SGT John F Plunkard US Army
properties.Info: Dedicated to United States Army Sergeant John Francis Plunkard, Frederick native and Frederick High School alumnus, who was killed in action in the Thua Thien province of Vietnam on April 8, 1968 while supporting the Vietnam War.

properties.Dedication_Signed: Dedicated to Donnie L Drewer
properties.Info: Dedicated to Donnie L. Drewer, Crisfield native, who gave 55 years of dedicated service to the MDOT State Highway Administration and directly influenced nearly every major roadway project in Dorchester, Somerset, Wicomico and Worcester counties from 1988 to 2016.

properties.Dedication_Signed: CPL Christopher J Coffland US Army Afghanistan Campaign Killed In Action 11-13-2009
properties.Info: Dedicated to United States Army Corporal Christopher James Coffland, Baltimore native, who was killed in action in Afghanistan on November 13, 2009 while supporting Operation Enduring Freedom.

properties.Dedication_Signed: SGT David James Smith USMC Afghanistan Campaign Killed In Action 1-26-10
properties.Info: Dedicated to United States Marine Corps Sergeant David James Smith, Frederick native, who was killed in action in the Helmand province of Afghanistan on January 26, 2010 while supporting Operation Enduring Freedom. This was the first bridge in Maryland dedicated to a fallen military member.

properties.Dedication_Signed: Dedicated to Delegate Theodore J Sophocleus
properties.Info: Dedicated to Theodore J. Sophocleus, prominent area political official and longtime public servant who represented District 32 in the Maryland House of Delegates from 1999 to 2018.

properties.Dedication_Signed: Dedicated to LCPL Matthew A Snyder
properties.Info: Dedicated to United States Marine Corps Lance Corporal Matthew A. Snyder, Finksburg native and Westminster High School alumnus, who was killed in action in the Anbar Province, Iraq on March 3, 2006 while supporting Operation Iraqi Freedom.

properties.Dedication_Signed: CPT John Ryan Dennison US Army Ranger -  Iraq Killed In Action 11-15-06
properties.Info: Dedicated to United States Army Captain John Ryan Dennison, Urbana High School alumnus, who was killed in action in Balad, Iraq on November 15, 2006 while supporting Operation Iraqi Freedom.

properties.Dedication_Signed: Dedicated to Champions of Voter Reform
properties.Info: Dedicated to the ten men of the North Dorchester Democratic Club who in 1986 successfully fought to change the statewide county council election process from at-large voting to a district voting system. The nearby Historical Freedom Shrine commemorates the amendment to the Maryland State Constitution and declares North Dorchester County as the “Birthplace of Voter Reform.”

properties.Dedication_Signed: SGT Sherwood Reynolds US Army Vietnam War Killed in Action 2-26-68
properties.Info: Dedicated to United States Army Sergeant Sherwood Reynolds, Prince Frederick native, who was killed in action on February 26, 1968 while supporting the Vietnam War. Sergeant Reynolds was the first service member from Calvert County to perish in combat in Vietnam.

properties.Dedication_Signed: A2C Gary Wayne Cosgrave USAF Vietnam War Killed in Action 5-12-67
properties.Info: Dedicated to United States Air Force Airman Second Class Gary Wayne Cosgrave, Frederick native, who was killed in action on May 12, 1967 while supporting the Vietnam War.

properties.Dedication_Signed: PFC C Franklin Brandenburg US Army - Vietnam War Killed In Action 08-05-67
properties.Info: Dedicated to United States Army Private First Class Sergeant Charles Franklin Brandenburg, Frederick native and Frederick High School alumnus, who was killed in action in the Quang Ngai Province of South Vietnam on August 5, 1967 while supporting the Vietnam War.

properties.Dedication_Signed: Dedicated to Officer Oliver Smith, Jr
properties.Info: Dedicated to Metropolitan Police Officer Oliver Wendell Smith, Jr. who was killed in the line of duty during a robbery while off duty on February 26, 1997.

properties.Dedication_Signed: Dedicated to LT (SEAL) Brendan Looney United States Navy
properties.Info: Dedicated to United States Navy Lieutenant (SEAL) John Brendan Aloysius Looney, United States Naval Academy alumnus and Owings Mills native, who was killed in action in Ayatalah Village, Afghanistan on September 21, 2010 while supporting Operation Enduring Freedom.

properties.Dedication_Signed: Dedicated to Senator James Ed DeGrange, Sr.
properties.Info: Dedicated to Senator James “Ed” DeGrange, Sr., prominent area political official who represented District 32 in the Maryland State Senate from 1999 to 2018.

properties.Dedication_Signed: Dedicated to PFC Russell Milberry United State Army
properties.Info: Dedicated to United States Army Private First Class Russell E. Milberry, Union Bridge native, who was killed in action in the Quang Tri province of Vietnam on January 31, 1968 while supporting the Vietnam War.

properties.Dedication_Signed: PFC James Moore Jr Paratrooper Vietnam Killed April 2, 1966
properties.Info: Dedicated to United States Army Private First Class James Moore, Jr., Baltimore native, who was killed in action on April 2, 1966 while supporting the Vietnam War.

properties.Dedication_Signed: Bridge Dedicated to Capt Michael S Lawlor United States Marine Corps
properties.Info: Dedicated to United States Marine Corps Captain Michael Seamus Lawlor, Timonium native and Towson University alum, who was killed in action along with 3 others marines during a military training accident in Camp Pendleton, California on January 22, 2004.

properties.Dedication_Signed: Dedicated to PS1 Xavier A Martin United States Navy
properties.Info: Dedicated to United States Navy Petty Officer First Class Xavier A. Martin, Halethorpe native, who perished along with 6 others on the U.S.S. Fitzgerald on June 17, 2017.

properties.Dedication_Signed: Joppa Road Bridge
properties.Info: Opened July 3, 1958 by Theodore R. McKeldin - Governor - State Roads Commission - Contractor: Williams Construction Co., Inc.

properties.Dedication_Signed: Bridge Dedicated to CPT Robert W Smith US Army - WWII
properties.Info: Dedicated to CPT Robert W Smith US Army, Harford County native, WWII veteran who landed at Normandy on D-Day, who died at the age of 97.

properties.Dedication_Signed: Bridge Dedicated to SGT Eric M Houck SSG Christopher O Moudry
properties.Info: Dedicated to US Army SGT Eric M. Houck, 25, of Baltimore who died from gunshot wounds in the Peka Valley of the Nangarhar Province in Afghanistan & US Army SSG Christopher O. Moudry, 31, of Baltimore; assigned to 7th Squadron, 10th Cavalry Regiment, 1st Brigade, 4th Infantry Division, Fort Hood, Texas; killed Oct. 4 after being attacked by enemy forces using small-arms fire and other weapons in Taji, Iraq.

properties.Dedication_Signed: Dedicated to Brianna N Freeman
properties.Info: In a car crash that took the life of 10 month old Brianna Nicole Freeman, Brianna gave the gift of life to 3 others; her liver to a 2 month old, her kidneys to a 57 year old & her heart to a 3 year old.

properties.Dedication_Signed: Dedicated to Chief Daniel Lister Queen Anne - Hillsboro VFC
properties.Info: The assistant chief of the Queen Anne-Hillsboro Volunteer Fire Company died while working on a scene of a head-on crash with several people entrapped in the wreckage (Aug 2018).

properties.Dedication_Signed: Dedicated to Sheriff Joseph Lee Somerville St. Mary's County
properties.Info: In 1977, Joseph Lee Somerville became both the first black sheriff in St. Mary’s County & in the state of Maryland when he was appointed by the governor to complete the previous sheriff’s term. Somerville went on to win the 1978 election for St. Mary’s County Sheriff, which then made him the first elected Black sheriff in St. Mary’s County & in Maryland as well.

properties.Dedication_Signed: Dedicated to Sheriff Joseph Lee Somerville St. Mary's County
properties.Info: In 1977, Joseph Lee Somerville became both the first black sheriff in St. Mary’s County & in the state of Maryland when he was appointed by the governor to complete the previous sheriff’s term. Somerville went on to win the 1978 election for St. Mary’s County Sheriff, which then made him the first elected Black sheriff in St. Mary’s County & in Maryland as well.

properties.Dedication_Signed: Dedicated to Earle S "Jock" Freedman
properties.Info: Earle "Jock" Freedman is a retired bridge engineer and the State of Maryland's "longest-serving" employee.[1][2] Freedman, affectionately known as "Jock", began his career at age 20 in 1950 following graduation from Johns Hopkins University.[3] For over 65 years, Freedman designed and maintained over 2,500 bridges for the Maryland State Highway Administration.

properties.Dedication_Signed: Dedicated to SPC Toccara Green United States Army
properties.Info: SPC Toccara Green died in Al Asad, Iraq, when multiple improvised explosive devices detonated near her unit during convoy operations. She was assigned to the 57th Transportation Company, 548th Corps Support Battalion, Fort Drum, New York. Died on August 14, 2005.

properties.Dedication_Signed: Designated as Piscataway Highway
Designated as Piscataway Highway
Dedicated to Hon Henry B "Hank" Heller
properties.Info: Henry B. “Hank” Heller, was a Democrat who represented Montgomery County in the House of Delegates for two dozen years. Heller, a longtime educator & union activist who served as president of the Montgomery County Education Association, was elected to represent District 19, which takes in portions of Silver Spring and unincorporated Rockville, in 1986.

properties.Dedication_Signed: Dedicated to Senator George C Edwards
properties.Info: Senator George Edwards was elected as the State Senator for Maryland District 1 in 2006, which covers Garrett County and parts of Washington and Allegany counties. He defeated Thomas Conlon, both of whom ran for the seat vacated by John J. Hafer who retired after 16 years in the State Senate. Edwards was first elected to the Maryland House of Delegates in 1982 and served for 24 years before running and winning the State Senate seat.

properties.Dedication_Signed: Dedicated to SGT Mujahid Ramzziddin
properties.Info: Sergeant Mujahid Ramzziddin was shot and killed at approximately 10:15 am while attempting to protect a female subject who was being attacked in a domestic violence incident. Sergeant Ramzziddin, who was off duty and lived nearby, attempted to protect the female and confronted the man who was armed with a shotgun.

properties.Dedication_Signed: Bridge Dedicated to Veterans of Cecil County
Fallen Heroes Bridge Dedicated to Mayor Gerald Wesley Donovan
properties.Info: Gerald Donovan served the citizens of the Town of Chesapeake Beach as the Mayor for 24 years (from 1984-2008), served as the President of the North Beach Volunteer Fire Department and has operated the Rod-N-Reel resort, which continues to serve as the largest employer in the Town.  Mr. Donovan also founded, and hosted, the successful Cancer Gala/ Celebration of Life raising over $5M for the American Cancer Society.

properties.Dedication_Signed: Dedicated to Senator Ed Reilly
properties.Info: The nine-mile portion of Maryland Route 450 from Crofton to Annapolis known as Defense Highway will be officially renamed after Reilly on October 25, 2023. Reilly worked on solutions to flooding along the road in 2018 & 2019.

properties.Dedication_Signed: Dedicated to Deputy Chief Sander B Cohen Office of the State Fire Marshall
properties.Info: Deputy Chief Sander B. Cohen was killed on December 8, 2017 at approximately 10:00 p.m. after stopping to assist FBI Supervisory Special Agent Carlos Wolff who had just been involved in single vehicle accident in the southbound lane of I-270 in the Rockville area.

properties.Dedication_Signed: Dedicated to the Honorable Karen Montgomery
properties.Info: Karen Slater Montgomery (born August 23, 1935) is an American politician and artist from the state of Maryland. She served as the state senator for the Maryland State Senate from Maryland's 14th District, which includes parts of Silver Spring, Burtonsville, Olney, Barnesville, Brookeville, Laytonsville and Damascus in Montgomery County.

properties.Dedication_Signed: Dedicated to Commissioner Clyde W Unglesbee
properties.Info: Mr. Unglesbee was a Town of Brookeville commissioner, charter president and life member of Olney Chamber of Commerce, member of the board of directors of: Montgomery General Hospital; Sandy Spring National Bank; Asbury Methodist Village; and Sandy Spring Museum, serving two years as chairman.

properties.Dedication_Signed: Dedicated to PFC Michael A Carter Jr United States Marine Corps
properties.Info: An 18-year-old Marine, who was back near his Maryland hometown recruiting for the Corps, died in a single-vehicle crash.  PFC. Michael Anthony Carter Jr. died Feb. 7 when his Toyota Camry hit a guardrail and went into a ditch, according to Delmarva Now.

properties.Dedication_Signed: Michael R Powers Libertytown VFD 6-25-2019
properties.Info: Mr. Michael R. Powers, 70, of Libertytown, passed away on Tuesday, June 25, 2019, in the line of duty while operating at an emergency incident scene.

properties.Dedication_Signed: Dedicated to Detective Jacai Colson Prince George's Co. Police Dept.
properties.Info: Corporal Jacai Colson was shot and killed while responding to an active shooter at the Prince George's County Police Department's District III police station at 7600 Barlowe Road on March 13, 2016.

properties.Dedication_Signed: Dedicated to PFC Donald H Selby Korean War MIA-POW
properties.Info: On the night of Feb. 11, 1951 Company K of the U.S. Army’s 3rd Battalion, 9th Regiment, 2nd Infantry Division was operating a task force alongside the Republic of Korea’s 16th Infantry Regiment to push back Chinese Communists during the Korean War. The incursion was brief, but deadly, according to the Defense POW/MIA Accounting Agency, and Private First Class Donald Harold Selby of Oriole was among those captured by the Chinese. After hostilities ended, repatriated POWs reported Pfc Selby died.

properties.Dedication_Signed: Dedicated to PFC Thurman B Ellis Army Rangers 1st Batt
properties.Info: Thurman B Ellis from Jarrettsville, MD served with distinction in WWII. A Private First Class in the US Army he was inducted into the service in February 1943 & received his basic training at Camp McCain, MS. He was assigned to the US Army Rangers Company C 1st Ranger Battalion which was part of a composite force of the 6615th Ranger Force (Provisional) with the 1st, 3rd, and 4th Ranger Battalions, 83rd Chemical Mortar Battalion, & the 509th Parachute Infantry Battalion.

properties.Dedication_Signed: Bridge Dedicated to PFC Charles W Harbert Killed in Action 5-12-1968
properties.Info: PFC Charles Harbert died from gunshot wounds received when hit by hostile small arms fire while on combat operations. He was a member of Company H, 2nd Bn., 502 Infantry, 101st Airborne Division.
